learning_objectives: Describe the mental health-mental illness continuum.
Identify types and characteristics of crises.
Understand ethical and legal responsibilities in mental health practice.
key_focus_areas: Legal responsibilities (e.g., Baker Act, duty to warn).

sections.title: Mental Health Continuum
sections.content: Mental health ranges from optimal well-being to severe illness. Individuals move along this spectrum throughout life based on biological, psychological, and social factors.

sections.title: Influences on Mental Health
sections.content: Protective and risk factors such as genetics, coping skills, trauma history, and social support determine vulnerability to mental illness or resilience.

sections.title: Crisis Intervention Fundamentals
sections.content: A crisis occurs when usual coping mechanisms fail, causing distress and functional impairment. Nursing priorities are ensuring safety, rapid assessment, and restoring equilibrium.

sections.title: Types of Crises and Nursing Roles
sections.content: Maturational, situational, and adventitious crises require different approaches. Nurses provide immediate support, help clients explore feelings, and connect them with resources.

sections.title: Models of Crisis Intervention
sections.content: Frameworks like the SAFER-R and Roberts’ seven-stage model guide assessment, problem solving, action planning, and follow-up.

sections.title: DSM-5 vs. Nursing Diagnoses
sections.content: DSM-5 provides medical classifications of mental disorders, while nursing diagnoses focus on human responses and guide holistic care planning.

sections.title: Legal and Ethical Responsibilities
sections.content: Nurses must consider duty to warn, involuntary treatment statutes like the Baker Act, and the principle of least restrictive environment when caring for clients in crisis.

sections.title: Basic Neurobiology
sections.content: Mental health conditions involve alterations in brain structure and neurochemistry. Understanding neurotransmitters and brain regions helps explain medication actions.

sections.title: Community Resources and Prevention
sections.content: Linking clients with community programs, support groups, and preventive services promotes recovery and helps maintain mental wellness.

sections.title: Conclusion
sections.content: Recognizing mental health as a continuum and responding effectively to crises are foundational skills. Ethical practice and knowledge of diagnoses support safe, compassionate care.
